name: Smart Contract Developer
oneLiner: Write, test, and deploy the code that runs on blockchain networks
dayInTheLife: Your day starts by reviewing a PR for a new token standard implementation. After unit tests pass, you dive into gas optimization — every byte matters. Then it's a deep work session implementing a new AMM feature. You end the day running a testnet deployment and reviewing security audit findings with the team.

juniorToSenior.junior: Write basic smart contracts from templates. Understand function calls and state changes. Can deploy to testnet. Needs code review for security.
juniorToSenior.mid: Design contract architecture. Optimize for gas. Implement complex DeFi logic. Mentor juniors. Participate in security reviews.
juniorToSenior.senior: Lead protocol design. Make architecture decisions for multiple contracts. Conduct security audits. Advise on protocol-level economics and incentives.

proofOfWorkTips: Deploy a simple ERC-20 or ERC-721 contract on Ethereum testnet
Build a basic DEX or lending protocol
Contribute to open-source contracts (OpenZeppelin, Aave, etc.)
Publish a security audit or findings report
Build a complex DeFi protocol with multiple interacting contracts

avgCompRange.note: Token grants (5-30% of ask) common at later-stage projects. Very variable based on location and project funding.

interviewQuestions: Explain reentrancy attacks and how you'd prevent them
Walk us through your approach to optimizing gas costs in a complex contract
How would you design a staking system with variable APY based on protocol revenue?
What's the difference between delegatecall and call, and when would you use each?
Describe a complex contract you've built and the security considerations you made

whoToFollowOnCT.why: Core Solidity dev, explains complex contract patterns
Security researcher, DeFi audits and exploits

name: Web3 Frontend Engineer
oneLiner: Build beautiful, fast interfaces that connect users to blockchain dapps
dayInTheLife: Start with Figma designs for a new token swap interface. Integrate wallet connection and test on mainnet fork. Debug a gas estimation issue with the smart contract team. Deploy to IPFS and verify testnet deployment works end-to-end. End with code review and accessibility pass.

juniorToSenior.junior: Implement UI from designs. Integrate wallet connectivity. Display blockchain data. Basic transaction flows. Needs support on architecture.
juniorToSenior.mid: Design component systems. Optimize rendering for frequent state changes. Build complex user flows. Mentoring. Performance optimization.
juniorToSenior.senior: Lead product direction. Architect scalable dapp interfaces. Make decisions on Web3UX patterns. Drive user research. Scale to millions of transactions.

proofOfWorkTips: Build a simple NFT minter or viewer app
Create a token swap interface (fork of Uniswap UI)
Build a wallet dashboard showing portfolios
Create a governance dapp with voting
Contribute to open-source dapp projects

avgCompRange.note: Token allocations typical. Startups offer 0.1-1% of projects.

interviewQuestions: Walk us through integrating MetaMask wallet connection
How would you handle transaction state and user feedback (pending/success/error)?
Describe your approach to optimizing re-renders in a React dapp
How do you manage private keys securely in a frontend application?
What's the difference between on-chain and off-chain data, and how would you display both?

whoToFollowOnCT.why: Wagmi library creator, best Web3 React hooks
Web3 UX and dapp design insights

name: Blockchain Researcher
oneLiner: Study protocol mechanics, analyze networks, and propose improvements
dayInTheLife: Morning: analyze on-chain data using Dune to understand liquidity patterns. Afternoon: write a proposal for protocol parameter changes based on research. Evening: review literature on MEV and prepare slides for team discussion.

juniorToSenior.junior: Analyze public data. Write blog posts explaining protocol mechanics. Basic data visualization. Needs guidance on research direction.
juniorToSenior.mid: Design experiments to test hypotheses. Publish research findings. Present at conferences. Identify protocol improvements.
juniorToSenior.senior: Set research agenda. Lead multi-person research initiatives. Influence protocol governance. Publish peer-reviewed work.

proofOfWorkTips: Publish analysis on Substack or Medium explaining DeFi mechanics
Create Dune dashboards analyzing protocol metrics
Contribute to protocol governance with data-driven proposals
Publish a research paper on a blockchain topic
Build a data tool for analyzing on-chain metrics

avgCompRange.note: Highly dependent on protocol backing. Grants more common than salaries for independent research.

interviewQuestions: Describe a research question you'd want to answer about any blockchain
How would you approach analyzing the economic incentives in a new protocol?
Walk us through your process for identifying and validating a protocol optimization
How do you stay current with blockchain research developments?
Describe a complex analysis you've done and your findings

whoToFollowOnCT.why: Legendary researcher, game theory and DeFi

name: NFT Designer
oneLiner: Create digital art, collections, and visual identities for blockchain projects
dayInTheLife: Begin with client kickoff discussing brand guidelines and target community. Create 20 NFT variations exploring different aesthetics. Get feedback and iterate. Design metadata and trait systems. Mint test collection and deploy. Network with community.

juniorToSenior.junior: Execute design briefs. Create NFT artwork. Understand trait systems. Needs direction on creative choices.
juniorToSenior.mid: Design full collections. Art direction for teams. Define visual identity. Community collaboration. Lead creative decisions.
juniorToSenior.senior: Establish design trends. Advise on collection strategy. Lead large creative initiatives. Build recognizable brands.

proofOfWorkTips: Design and mint your own NFT collection
Create art for an open-source NFT project
Design NFTs for a cause or community
Build a generative art NFT project
Create a complete visual identity for a blockchain project

avgCompRange.note: Highly variable. Success depends on collection sales. Royalties critical to income.

interviewQuestions: Walk us through your design process for a new NFT collection
How do you approach creating utility in visual design?
Tell us about a project you're proud of and why
How do you stay inspired and current with design trends?
Describe your experience with community feedback and iteration

name: Community Manager
oneLiner: Build and nurture engaged communities around blockchain projects
dayInTheLife: Monitor Discord for questions and escalate technical issues. Post daily updates on Twitter. Review analytics on engagement metrics. Host a community AMA. Moderate heated discussion about protocol governance. Plan next month's community event.

juniorToSenior.junior: Monitor channels. Respond to questions. Enforce rules. Create basic content. Needs guidance on strategy.
juniorToSenior.mid: Set community strategy. Create engagement programs. Analyze metrics. Lead events. Build partnerships.
juniorToSenior.senior: Own community vision and growth. Direct multi-channel strategy. Represent to governance. Identify trends early.

proofOfWorkTips: Build and grow an active Discord or Telegram community
Create viral Twitter content about a protocol
Organize community events or AMAs
Build community tools or dashboards
Document community insights and trends

avgCompRange.note: Often supplemented with tokens. Bounties and retroactive airdrops common.

interviewQuestions: Describe a community challenge you've faced and how you solved it
What metrics do you track to measure community health?
How do you handle misinformation or FUD in community channels?
Tell us about a successful community event or initiative you led
How do you balance growth with community quality?

name: Web3 Product Manager
oneLiner: Shape product strategy for blockchain applications
dayInTheLife: Analyze user behavior data from Dune and Amplitude. Interview power users about pain points. Write product specs for new features. Prioritize roadmap with engineering. Present product direction to the board. Review competitive landscape.

juniorToSenior.junior: Own specific features. Gather user feedback. Basic roadmap planning. Needs mentorship on strategy.
juniorToSenior.mid: Own product area. Define strategy and roadmap. Lead cross-functional work. Analyze metrics.
juniorToSenior.senior: Define product vision. Set company direction. Make key strategic bets. Lead fundraising narrative.

proofOfWorkTips: Write detailed product case studies on Substack
Contribute to protocol governance with product insights
Build a small product from concept to launch
Create product teardowns of successful dapps
Show growth and engagement metrics you've driven

avgCompRange.note: Token grants (1-5%) very standard. Heavily equity-weighted at startups.

interviewQuestions: What's a product you love in crypto and why?
How would you approach finding product-market fit for a new protocol?
Walk us through your process for prioritizing features
Describe a product decision you made based on data
How do you balance short-term growth with long-term vision?

whoToFollowOnCT.why: Vision for crypto products and networks
